content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 2.1 WARN — description.txt claims "Built-in ad blocker", "AI-powered suggestions", and "Works fully offline", but ContentView.swift (App/ContentView.swift:1-19) only implements a plain local in-memory task list with no ad-blocking, ML/AI, or persistence/offline-storage code, and no other Swift files or imports (e.g. CoreML, NetworkExtension, ContentBlocker) are present.

Pierre: Apple's reviewers actively test marketing claims against real app behavior, and unsubstantiated claims like "AI-powered" or "ad blocker" are a common 2.1/2.3 rejection reason since they mislead users about functionality. Here the code is a trivial to-do list with no AI logic, no content-blocking extension, and no explicit offline persistence (state is just in-memory `@State`, lost on relaunch). Either implement the claimed features (e.g. add a content blocker extension, a real ML/heuristic suggestion engine, and persistent local storage) or rewrite the description to match what the app actually does.
